name: BTC Inventory Reset Reclaim v1
thesis: BTC 4h is the best-fit primary lane for testing inventory-reset continuation after a flush-and-reclaim rather than static oversold bounce logic. The edge should come from reclaim-after-flush behavior with broad participation thresholds and staged management.
invalidation_condition: Abort if reclaim setups remain too rare, if direction confirmation conflicts with the recovery logic, or if later exit slices fail to pay for the idea after costs.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A sharp flush resets inventory, price reclaims value, and continuation follows because the panic move was over-extended.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Transition/chop mixtures often reward reclaim-after-flush structure more than passive oversold buying because acceptance back into value matters more than the first touch.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: BTC 4h is a strong primary lane because the reset needs enough amplitude to matter but still benefits from multi-bar continuation once value is reclaimed.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the market remains balance-dominated instead of transitioning, reclaim moves may bounce and stall before later targets.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries populate but continuation is shallow, reduce runner ambition before tightening the recovery trigger.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; reclaim and directional confirmation align after the reset
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad reset thresholds instead of rare extreme events
